body.title: feat: test generation — Igor writes tests for his own subsystems
body.result: cancelled — Igor coding retired (D-worker-daemon-retired-2026-05-25); Igor test generation shelved
body.description:
## Imported from GH#209 (created 2026-03-13, open) — depends on #207 (hot-reload) + #208 (introspection)

Igor can write and run tests for his own subsystems — not because we asked, but because he noticed something worth verifying.

Pattern: Igor observes an anomaly via introspection (e.g. tier.3.5 latency spiked at 14:00 three days running) → he writes a targeted test, isolating that path with known input → tests land in `tests/` as real Python files, auto-committed via self_edit pipeline → he can also generate regression tests after fixing a bug.

Requires: hot module reload (#207), full introspection (#208), fixture DB support (from live testing infrastructure #206 Track 1), `run_test(test_path)` tool.

Key principle: no mocks. Tests run against real systems with fixture data in the DB.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/run_test.py (new — execute a test file, return stdout/stderr); wild_igor/igor/tools/self_edit.py (test-write integration via existing patch primitives); tests/fixtures/ (fixture DB support); cross-cuts hot-reload (#207) + introspection (#208) when those land.

**Design rules:** docs-in-code; test-plan-or-why-not; no-mocks-policy (Igor's tests run against real systems with fixture data).

**Scope boundary:** IN — run_test tool + Igor habit that authors targeted tests via self_edit. OUT — Igor mocking; coverage targeting (Igor decides what to test, not "achieve N%"); test-runner refactor.

**Test plan:** tests/test_run_test_tool.py — execute a fixture test file, assert stdout/stderr captured. Integration test gated on hot-reload + introspection landing.

**Status:** blocked on #207 + #208 — re-evaluate when those ship.
